title: Tabbouleh (Lebanese Parsley Salad)

ingredients.name: fresh parsley, very finely chopped
visual_checks: use curly or flat-leaf; this is the main ingredient, not a garnish
ingredients.notes: use curly or flat-leaf; this is the main ingredient, not a garnish

subs.name: extra mint and coriander leaves in equal parts
subs.notes: flat-leaf parsley gives a better result than coriander but the salad still works

ingredients.name: spring onion or shallot, finely sliced
bulgur wheat or fine semolina (rava)
visual_checks: soak in cold water for 20 minutes; bulgur is traditional but rava works in India
ingredients.notes: soak in cold water for 20 minutes; bulgur is traditional but rava works in India

subs.name: 3 tbsp fine broken wheat (dalia) or skip grain entirely for a gluten-free salad
subs.notes: authentic Lebanese tabbouleh uses very little grain

subs.name: any light neutral oil
subs.notes: olive oil gives the authentic flavour

steps.text: Soak the bulgur wheat (or rava) in cold water for 20 minutes until just softened. Drain thoroughly through a fine sieve and squeeze out any excess water — wet grain will make the salad soggy.
While the grain soaks, wash and dry the parsley and mint well. Bundle the herbs and chop very finely using a sharp knife — the knife technique rather than a blender preserves texture.
Dice the tomatoes and remove the seeds and excess juice before adding to the bowl. Finely slice the spring onions, including the green tops.
Combine the drained grain, parsley, mint, tomato, spring onion and cucumber (if using) in a large bowl. Drizzle over the lemon juice and olive oil, season with salt, and toss gently to combine.

Taste and adjust lemon and salt — tabbouleh should be bright and assertively lemony. Serve immediately or rest for 10 minutes for the flavours to meld; do not refrigerate for long or the herbs will darken.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: rava → 3 tbsp fine broken wheat (dalia) or skip grain entirely for a gluten-free salad (authentic Lebanese tabbouleh uses very little grain); parsley → extra mint and coriander leaves in equal parts (flat-leaf parsley gives a better result than coriander but the salad still works); olive-oil → any light neutral oil (olive oil gives the authentic flavour)
indian_kitchen_adaptation: Use rava (fine sooji) soaked in cold water as the grain component — bulgur wheat may need to be sourced from a specialty or online store. Parsley (parsley/ajmoda) is available at larger vegetable markets or can be ordered online. No cooking needed — just chop, mix and serve.
